prompt: A hyper-realistic, uncanny architectural photograph of a colossal brutalist stone monument rising high into an overcast sky. The overall global shape of the massive structure undeniably points upwards, ascending to a towering peak. However, the entire surface of the structure is constructed from distinct, clearly separated stone ramps. Every single individual ramp visibly and steeply slopes downwards. The local downward slopes perfectly contradict the global upward ascent, creating an impossible, mind-bending optical illusion. High contrast, stark shadows, photorealistic textures of weathered concrete. No text, no numbers, no people, no signs, no floating diagrams.
visual_logic: The monument is built entirely of downward-sloping local ramps, yet the aggregated global structure ascends upwards. This spatial impossibility physically embodies how subgroup trends can completely reverse when combined.
